name: Canyonlands Dark Sky Park
description: IDA-certified International Dark Sky Park (2015). Among the darkest skies in the contiguous US; central panel of the Colorado Plateau dark sky cluster.
_notes: Centroid is approximate (Island in the Sky). Certification: 2015. Park polygon already in pilot_regions; this row gives the recommendation engine a discoverable POI.

name: Dead Horse Point Dark Sky Park
description: IDA-certified International Dark Sky Park (2016). Sweeping Colorado River canyon overlook with exceptional dark skies; right outside Canyonlands.

name: Natural Bridges Dark Sky Park
description: First IDA-certified International Dark Sky Park in the world (2007); Bortle 2 skies. Three natural bridges visible by day; world-class night sky.
_notes: Original 2007 certification. Just outside the strict 50mi radius (~64mi) but pilot-relevant.

name: Hovenweep Dark Sky Park
description: IDA-certified International Dark Sky Park (2014). Ancestral Puebloan archaeology by day, exceptional dark skies by night; remote and uncrowded.
_notes: Certification: 2014. Outside the strict 50mi radius but pilot-relevant.

name: Goosenecks Dark Sky Park
description: IDA-certified International Dark Sky Park (2017). Tightly meandering San Juan River entrenched 1,000 ft below the rim; minimal light pollution.
_notes: Certification: 2017. Outside Moab radius but pilot-relevant.

name: Rainbow Bridge Dark Sky Park
description: IDA-certified Dark Sky Sanctuary (2019). World's largest known natural bridge; reachable only by boat or long backcountry hike. Sacred to Diné, Hopi, Paiute.
_notes: Certification: 2019. Far outside Moab radius (~120mi) but a known cluster member.
